title: Cross-Merchant Card Rotation with Elevated Multi-Merchant Counter Aggregation
narrative: Compromised card credentials are distributed across multiple fraudsters or automated scripts operating at different merchant locations simultaneously. The card appears at an unusually high number of distinct merchants within a rolling window, visible through C8 (distinct merchant counter) and C11 (aggregate card-merchant activity). Individual transaction amounts and timing appear normal to avoid velocity triggers, but the card's usage pattern reveals coordination across merchant channels that no legitimate cardholder would exhibit. This pattern is common when card details are sold on carding forums and used by multiple buyers before the card is frozen. The diversity of merchant categories and the lack of repeat merchant behavior distinguish this from normal shopping patterns.
indicators: C8 > 29 (p90), indicating card used at more than 29 distinct merchants in rolling window
C11 > 27 (p90), elevated aggregate card-merchant activity counter beyond typical cardholder patterns
C13 > 24 (p90), high distinct-category counter showing card used across many different MCC codes
card1_distinct_addr1 > 8, card associated with numerous different billing addresses in recent history
C14 between 2 and 7 (p75-p90 range), moderate repeat-merchant rate inconsistent with high C8 value
TransactionAmt between 50 and 200, individual amounts within normal range masking aggregate abuse
D1 = 0 or D2 = 0, indicating first-time device-card pairing despite established card history
indicator_explanations: The card has been presented at an unusually large number of different merchants recently
Aggregated merchant-card activity shows far more diversity than a typical cardholder's shopping behavior
The card has been used across many different merchant category codes in a short period
Multiple different billing addresses have been associated with this card in recent transactions
Very few repeat visits to the same merchant despite high overall merchant count
Each individual purchase amount appears reasonable and does not trigger amount-based alerts
The device has never been seen with this card before despite card's established transaction history
summary: The card appears at an abnormally high number of distinct merchants across diverse categories within a short window, while individual transaction behavior remains unremarkable. The lack of repeat-merchant patterns combined with elevated cross-merchant counters indicates coordinated multi-party use of stolen credentials.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders develop stable merchant-usage patterns with repeat visits and category preferences. When a card suddenly appears at dozens of different merchants across many categories with minimal repeat behavior, it indicates the credentials have been distributed to multiple fraudsters operating independently across different merchant channels.
